announcements.title: AI Tutor Access — Claude API Key or SciQuant Cloud
announcements.message:
There are two ways to get high-quality AI tutoring inside SciQuant this semester.

1. Your own Claude API key (recommended for the full semester)
You are encouraged to get your own personal Claude API key and use Claude as your tutor inside SciQuant. Sign up at console.anthropic.com, then go to Settings → Billing to add credit (a card or similar payment method is required). About $5–10 of credit is typically enough for a full semester of regular use. Full setup steps are in the app under Settings → AI Backend → Claude API.

2. SciQuant Cloud AI service (free, unit-provided)
If you'd rather not set up your own key, you can use the SciQuant Cloud AI service instead — during the first couple of weeks of semester, and during lab sessions for the rest of the semester. In the app, go to Settings → AI Backend → SciQuant Cloud and enter this Server URL:

https://sciquant-tutor-proxy.scie1500.workers.dev

Leave the Auth Token field blank.

Please keep this URL to yourself — don't post it publicly or share it outside this unit. The cost of the service depends on how much it is used, so unnecessary sharing could affect availability for everyone in the class.

announcements.title: Welcome to SCIE1500 — Semester 2, 2026
announcements.message:
My name is Atakelty Hailu, and I'm excited to guide you through this unit.

• Teaching begins Monday 20 July. Please read the unit outline in full before Week 1.
• Download and install the SciQuant Assistant app — the link is in the LMS. All lessons, practice questions, and lab notebooks are delivered through the app.
• All assignment submissions and grading remain on the LMS.

What is new this semester:

The lab programme has been redesigned around Problem Briefs — realistic scientific scenarios you work on in permanent groups of 5–7. Each week your group submits a completed worksheet, but uploads are batched: worksheets are due in four batches (not weekly), on 11 Aug, 8 Sep, 29 Sep, and 27 Oct (all at 11:59pm). Every student must upload their own copy to LMS by the relevant batch deadline.

From Weeks 3 to 9, two groups present the previous week's work at the start of each lab (10 min, all members speak). Each group presents exactly twice. The final three labs (Weeks 10–12) replace the standard format with structured exam preparation: a mock exam workshop, a peer review exchange, and a gallery walk capstone.

The lectures are also new: science and mathematics content is now delivered as a single integrated set of lessons inside the SciQuant app.

Welcome — I look forward to working with you this semester.
Atakelty (Unit Coordinator)
